state_represented: After a perfect interaction round in which all public and private candidate information has been exchanged, attributed, integrated, and evaluated.

task.goal: Choose the best candidate for a long-distance pilot position at an airline.

decision.rationale: Candidate C is the only candidate whose complete evidence set clearly covers reliability, calmness under pressure, technical competence, attention, quick correct decisions, and crew-oriented cooperation. The public drawbacks remain relevant, but the full private evidence makes Candidate C the broadest and strongest fit.

evidence_for.text: Candidate A can anticipate dangerous situations.
Candidate A is able to see complex connections.
Candidate A has excellent spatial vision.
Candidate A has very good leadership qualities.

evidence_against.text: Candidate A is sometimes not good at taking criticism.
Candidate A can be unorganized.
Candidate A is regarded as a show-off.
Candidate A is regarded as being not open to new ideas.
Candidate A is unfriendly.
Candidate A eats unhealthily.

Candidate A.role_fit: Strong cognitive, spatial, anticipation, and leadership evidence, but weak cooperation, adaptability, and organization.
Candidate A.assessment: Not optimal because several agents' private information reveals direct interpersonal and adaptability concerns.

evidence_for.text: Candidate B handles stress very well.
Candidate B is good at assessing weather conditions.
Candidate B has excellent computer skills.

evidence_against.text: Candidate B can be grumpy.
Candidate B can be uncooperative.
Candidate B has a relatively weak memory for numbers.
Candidate B makes nasty remarks about his colleagues.
Candidate B is regarded as pretentious.
Candidate B sometimes adopts the wrong tone when communicating.

Candidate B.role_fit: Strong reliability, stress tolerance, weather assessment, and computer evidence, but repeated direct cooperation and communication negatives plus a numeric-memory weakness.
Candidate B.assessment: Plausible from public information alone, but not optimal once private information is shared.

evidence_for.text: Candidate C can make correct decisions quickly.
Candidate C is known to be 100% reliable.
Candidate C creates a positive atmosphere with his crew.
Candidate C keeps calm in a crisis.
Candidate C puts concern for others above everything.
Candidate C has excellent attention skills.

evidence_against.text: Candidate C has difficulty communicating ideas.
Candidate C is regarded as egocentric.
Candidate C is not very willing to further his education.

Candidate C.role_fit: Best balanced fit across reliability, calmness, technical understanding, attention, quick correct decisions, and crew-oriented cooperation.
Candidate C.assessment: Correct candidate. Public drawbacks remain relevant, but the full group evidence gives Candidate C the broadest and strongest overall role fit.

evidence_for.text: Candidate D responds to unexpected events adequately.
Candidate D can concentrate very well.
Candidate D solves problems extremely well.

evidence_against.text: Candidate D is regarded as arrogant.
Candidate D has relatively weak leadership skills.
Candidate D is regarded as a know-it-all.
Candidate D has a hot temper.
Candidate D is considered moody.
Candidate D is regarded as a loner.

Candidate D.role_fit: Strong concentration, problem-solving, responsibility, and response to unexpected events, but poor cooperation, weak leadership, and emotional/interpersonal risk.
Candidate D.assessment: Not optimal because safety-critical teamwork evidence is substantially weaker than Candidate C's.

agent_1.main_reason: Candidate C combines 100% reliability and positive crew atmosphere with the public evidence of quick correct decisions.
evidence_shared: Candidate C is known to be 100% reliable.
Candidate C creates a positive atmosphere with his crew.
Candidate A is sometimes not good at taking criticism.
Candidate A can be unorganized.
Candidate B can be grumpy.
Candidate B can be uncooperative.
Candidate D is regarded as arrogant.
Candidate D has relatively weak leadership skills.

agent_2.main_reason: Candidate C adds calmness in crisis and complicated-technology understanding, while B and D have serious interpersonal risks.
evidence_shared: Candidate C keeps calm in a crisis.
Candidate C understands complicated technology.
Candidate A is regarded as a show-off.
Candidate A is regarded as being not open to new ideas.
Candidate B has a relatively weak memory for numbers.
Candidate B makes nasty remarks about his colleagues.
Candidate D is regarded as a know-it-all.
Candidate D has a hot temper.

agent_3.main_reason: Candidate C adds concern for others and excellent attention, while A, B, and D have additional cooperation concerns.
evidence_shared: Candidate C puts concern for others above everything.
Candidate C has excellent attention skills.
Candidate A is unfriendly.
Candidate A eats unhealthily.
Candidate B is regarded as pretentious.
Candidate B sometimes adopts the wrong tone when communicating.
Candidate D is considered moody.
Candidate D is regarded as a loner.

important_agreements: All agents should converge on Candidate C once the full hidden-profile information is shared.
Candidate C has the strongest combined evidence for reliability, calmness under pressure, technical competence, attention, correct decisions, and positive crew interaction.
Candidate B looks strong from public information but loses its advantage after private cooperation and numeric-memory concerns are integrated.
Candidate A and Candidate D have strong technical or cognitive signals, but both carry multiple safety-relevant interpersonal or adaptability drawbacks.
important_tensions: Candidate C has public drawbacks: difficulty communicating ideas, egocentrism, and low willingness for further education.
The core evaluative tension is whether those drawbacks outweigh Candidate C's broad private strengths. In the full evidence set, they do not.
uncertainties: No missing candidate information remains within the configured task.
Residual uncertainty is limited to weighting the known drawbacks, not to factual incompleteness.

what_would_change_the_decision: New valid task evidence showing that Candidate C's communication, egocentrism, or low willingness for further education is more safety-critical than the repeated cooperation risks of the alternatives.

emerging_group_view.next_step_focus: No further information exchange is required after the perfect round. The appropriate next step is unanimous selection of Candidate C.
